data_in.description: task_002.expected_outputs[0]: SummarizedExperiment object with 'ratio_corrected' assay containing batch-corrected Compound/Internal Standard ratios
Pre-processed SummarizedExperiment object output from doAnalysis

data_out.description: Plots subdirectory containing quality-control visualization files
Reports subdirectory containing tab-delimited text files and Excel exports

domain_knowledge: The cautionRSD and nonReportableRSD parameters define thresholds for compound reliability: compounds with relative standard deviation above cautionRSD (15%) are flagged for caution, and those above nonReportableRSD (30%) are marked as unreliable.
The backgroundPercent parameter (40%) sets the threshold for identifying background signal intensity relative to peak intensity, used to filter weak or noisy measurements.

Quality reports in mzQuality include both visual plots (for human inspection) and tab-delimited text files (for downstream computational analysis) alongside Excel exports for stakeholder communication.

The assays parameter specifies which assay type to report on; 'area' refers to the integrated area under the chromatographic peak, a standard quantification metric in liquid chromatography–mass spectrometry.

direct_checks: verify file_exists: output directory contains 'Plots' subdirectory
verify file_exists: output directory contains 'Reports' subdirectory
verify file_format_is: all files in Plots subdirectory are image files (PNG, PDF, or similar graphics format)
verify file_format_is: all files in Reports subdirectory are tab-delimited text files (.txt or .tsv)

verify script_runs: createReports() function executes without error when called with parameters makeSummaryReport=TRUE, makeCompoundReport=TRUE, backgroundPercent=40, cautionRSD=15, nonReportableRSD=30, assays='area'
verify row_count_equals: summary report and compound report files are non-empty (contain at least header row plus one data row)
expert_review: Verify that generated plots are visually coherent and match documented mzQuality output styles for quality control visualization
Verify that report content appropriately reflects the specified RSD thresholds (cautionRSD=15, nonReportableRSD=30) in flagging or categorizing compounds

evidence_snippets.text: [results] Does the createReports function generate the expected directory structure with Plots and Reports sub-folders and the correct file types when called with specified parameters?: 'The `createReports` function creates a folder containing the results of the analysis. The results are separated by *Plots* and *Reports*.'

[results] The createReports function creates a folder containing analysis results organized into separate Plots and Reports sub-folders.: 'The `createReports` function creates a folder containing the results of the analysis. The results are separated by *Plots* and *Reports*.'
[other] Pre-processed SummarizedExperiment object output from doAnalysis: 'The `buildExperiment` function will then take the data and create an experiment object that can be used for analysis.'
[other] Plots subdirectory containing quality-control visualization files: 'an export will be made that contains the mentioned plots, tab-delimited text files'
[other] Reports subdirectory containing tab-delimited text files and Excel exports: 'tab-delimited text files for programmatic access and a human-friendly Excel export'
[other] mzQuality: 'mzQuality requires a specific format for the input data.'
[intro] SummarizedExperiment: 'Internally, mzQuality uses Bioconductors' *SummarizedExperiment* object to store the data.'

cmd: library(mzQuality); library(SummarizedExperiment); exp <- readRDS('{inputs.experiment_object}'); createReports(exp, makeSummaryReport=TRUE, makeCompoundReport=TRUE, backgroundPercent=40, cautionRSD=15, nonReportableRSD=30, assays='area'); if (!dir.exists('Plots') || !dir.exists('Reports')) stop('Missing output directories'); plots_files <- list.files('Plots', full.names=FALSE); reports_files <- list.files('Reports', full.names=FALSE); if (length(plots_files) == 0 || length(reports_files) == 0) stop('Output directories are empty'); cat('SUCCESS: createReports completed with output validation\n')
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

expected_outputs: Plots subdirectory containing quality-control visualization files
Reports subdirectory containing tab-delimited text files and Excel exports
finding: The createReports function creates a folder containing analysis results organized into separate Plots and Reports sub-folders.
inputs: task_002.expected_outputs[0]: SummarizedExperiment object with 'ratio_corrected' assay containing batch-corrected Compound/Internal Standard ratios
Pre-processed SummarizedExperiment object output from doAnalysis
methodology_summary: Load the pre-processed SummarizedExperiment object from the mzQuality analysis pipeline.
Invoke createReports with summary and compound report flags enabled, quality thresholds (backgroundPercent=40, cautionRSD=15, nonReportableRSD=30), and assay specification (area).
Generate Plots subdirectory containing quality-control visualizations and Reports subdirectory containing text and Excel files.
Validation: Confirm directory structure exists with expected subdirectories and file extensions (*.png in Plots; *.txt and *.xlsx in Reports).
research_question: Does the createReports function generate the expected directory structure with Plots and Reports sub-folders and the correct file types when called with specified parameters?
review_questions: Is the research question correctly identified and scoped?

task_description: Call createReports on an analysed mzQuality experiment object with parameters makeSummaryReport=TRUE, makeCompoundReport=TRUE, backgroundPercent=40, cautionRSD=15, nonReportableRSD=30, and assays='area', then verify that the expected Plots and Reports directory structure and file types are generated.
task_objective: Execute createReports with specified parameters and verify the output directory contains correctly organized Plots and Reports sub-folders with expected file types.

task_outputs.label: Output directory containing Plots and Reports subdirectories

title: Reproduce the createReports file-system export from the analysed experiment
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: SummarizedExperiment.
workflow_description: 1. Load a pre-processed SummarizedExperiment object created by buildExperiment and doAnalysis from the mzQuality package. 2. Call the createReports function with the specified parameters (makeSummaryReport=TRUE, makeCompoundReport=TRUE, backgroundPercent=40, cautionRSD=15, nonReportableRSD=30, assays='area'). 3. Verify that the output directory contains a Plots subdirectory with quality-control visualizations and a Reports subdirectory with tab-delimited text files and human-friendly Excel exports. 4. Confirm that all expected report files are present and in the correct format.
